id.description: Workflow identifier; must match the directory name.

summary.description: One or two sentence description of the workflow.

type.description: release-please release-type for this package (e.g. simple).

major.description: Current released major version line for this workflow, cross-validated against the major of its release-please manifest version. It is 0 while the workflow is pre-1.0 (the 0.x pre-completion contract); moving-major-tag automation and moving-major consumer references activate only once this reaches 1. Bump it to 1 in the same change that bumps the manifest baseline to 1.0.0.

runtime-jobs.description: Additional runner jobs that also materialize the DevFlows runtime scripts.

checkout-jobs.description: Additional runner jobs that also receive the injected checkout channel step. Requires checkout: true. None may equal io.job.

artifact-download-jobs.description: Additional runner jobs that also receive the injected artifact-download channel step (granted actions: read). Requires artifact-download: true. None may equal io.job.

setupFileEntry.description: A file placed into the checkout before the setup artifact is uploaded. Exactly one of content, source-path, or content-base64 supplies the bytes.

content.description: Inline UTF-8 text written to path.

source-path.description: Workspace-relative path (no '..') of a file in the checkout, copied verbatim to path. Use for binary or large payloads such as a prebuilt wheel.

content-base64.description: Base64-encoded bytes decoded and written to path. Use for small inline binary payloads.

expect.description: Expected outcome of the scenario. 'success' (default) calls the reusable workflow and asserts it succeeded. 'validation-failure' is a negative-path scenario that does NOT call the reusable workflow: instead the generated job checks the repository out and runs the target workflow's input-validation script (workflows/<id>/scripts/validate-inputs.py) directly, with the env the workflow's validate step would set (its inputs.* mapping, filled from this scenario's inputs and the workflow input defaults). The scenario passes when that script rejects the inputs (nonzero exit) and fails if it unexpectedly accepts them. This name is deliberately honest: it tests the input-validation script layer in CI, not a full reusable-call failure end to end (that is a future sandbox-repo capability). Because it is a plain job it runs under act, so it supports both local and hosted runs. It may not declare assertions, artifact, setup-artifact, mutation, writeback-payload, or cleanup.

failure-message-contains.description: Only valid with expect: validation-failure. When set, the scenario additionally requires that the rejected validation script's captured stdout/stderr contains this substring, so a scenario can pin the specific rejection message. Omit to accept any nonzero exit.
